meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Freelancers: No-Code to Pro-Level Guide
content.seo_description: Stop guessing. Learn to implement professional analytics without coding experience using Antigravity and Claude Code. A high-efficiency checklist for freelancers.
content.answer_block: You don't need a CS degree to master data. By leveraging AI-driven CLI tools like Claude Code and abstraction layers like Antigravity, you can inject sophisticated event tracking into any stack. This workflow automates the boilerplate, allowing you to ship production-ready analytics in minutes, not days, while ensuring total data accuracy.
content.intro:
The client meeting went well, but the silence following the launch is deafening. Without a data loop, you are flying blind, unable to prove your value or optimize the user journey. The gap between 'it works' and 'it converts' is analytics, yet most freelancers avoid it, fearing the complexity of SDKs, event schemas, and database triggers.

Today, we dismantle that barrier. We are moving beyond drag-and-drop plugins toward a professional, code-assisted implementation that runs on autopilot. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, you will bridge the technical divide, transforming your client projects into high-performance, data-driven assets that command higher retainers.

steps.title: Define the Event Schema
steps.description: Before touching code, define what matters. Identify the 3-5 critical user actions that drive business value for your client.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, JSON-based event schema for a SaaS funnel tracking signups, trial starts, and feature activations. Ensure the structure is extensible for future custom properties.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a schema.json file defining events for user_signup, trial_started, and feature_clicked with standard metadata fields'.

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Middleware
steps.description: Create a centralized wrapper to handle event dispatching. This prevents hardcoding SDK calls throughout your codebase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a modular analytics client wrapper in TypeScript that accepts event names and payloads, then dispatches them to a placeholder analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate an analytics.ts service file with a trackEvent method that logs to console for development and prepares for production SDK integration.'

steps.title: Automate Event Injection
steps.description: Use AI to identify where to place tracking calls in your existing UI components without manual searching.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scan the current project structure and identify the optimal locations to call the analytics.trackEvent function for user interactions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze current directory and suggest specific line insertions for analytics tracking in buttons and form submissions.'

steps.title: Implement Client-Side Hooks
steps.description: Wrap your primary interactive components with analytics triggers to capture user behavior automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React custom hook named useAnalytics that simplifies event firing from any functional component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create hooks/useAnalytics.ts and update the Login component to use the hook for tracking submission attempts.'

steps.title: Validate and Debug Data Flow
steps.description: Ensure data is flowing correctly by running a simulation suite before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test suite that simulates user clicks and verifies that the analytics middleware receives and formats the events correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Run a test script to simulate 5 user events and log the payload output to verify correct schema adherence.'

pro_tips: Always use a centralized event registry (a JSON/TS file) to avoid 'string typing' errors in your analytics calls.
Implement a 'dry-run' mode in your analytics service that logs to the browser console during local dev to avoid polluting production data.
Tag every event with a 'version' property so you can track schema changes over time without breaking historical data.
If using third-party SDKs, always proxy them through your own middleware to maintain total control over user privacy and data scrubbing.
Use AI to generate a 'Data Dictionary' PDF for your client; it makes you look like a senior data architect and justifies higher project fees.
content.next_steps: Once your tracking is live, connect your output to a visualization tool like Metabase or Looker Studio to create a client dashboard that visualizes the ROI of your work.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping data-backed products that your clients will love. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
